top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The world's biggest story tonight is a drone strike that sparked a fire at the perimeter of the UAE's Barakah nuclear plant — the first time a working nuclear site has been hit in the Iran war, and a red flag for the region. Diplomacy is being tested in parallel: Putin floated that the Ukraine war may be ending, Zelenskyy flatly disagreed, and Trump-Xi talks in Beijing point toward a tariff truce even as tensions simmer underneath. Closer to home, the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was extended by 45 days but Israeli strikes still killed five in southern Lebanon the same day.

stories.headline: Drone strike sparks fire at UAE's Barakah nuclear plant — a first in the Iran war
stories.summary: A drone hit a generator on the perimeter of Abu Dhabi's Barakah nuclear plant on Sunday, starting a fire that did not breach the inner site or affect radiation levels. The UAE said its defences intercepted two other drones launched from its western border and has opened an investigation. The IAEA's Rafael Grossi called any military activity threatening nuclear safety 'unacceptable' and urged maximum restraint.

stories.headline: Putin says Ukraine war 'coming to an end' — Zelenskyy strongly disagrees
stories.summary: President Putin suggested the war may be wrapping up and floated meeting Zelenskyy in a third country if a deal is reached. Zelenskyy publicly pushed back, saying Russia 'has no intention of ending this war' and that Ukraine is bracing for more attacks. The mixed messaging comes as Russia launched a record 8,000+ drones in April — the highest monthly total since the 2022 invasion.

stories.headline: Trump-Xi Beijing summit — analysts expect a tariff truce extension
stories.summary: Presidents Trump and Xi met at the Great Hall of the People last week to discuss tariffs, Taiwan, rare earths and the Iran war. Markets are betting the existing truce will harden into a formal deal, but US-China tariffs still average ~48% — roughly 15x pre-2018 levels. Xi warned that mishandling Taiwan would put the relationship in 'great jeopardy'.

stories.headline: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 45 days — but Israeli strikes kill 5 the same day
stories.summary: After Washington-brokered talks, Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their fragile ceasefire by 45 days. Hours later, Israeli air attacks on southern Lebanon killed at least five people and wounded more than a dozen. Hezbollah opposes direct talks while Israel continues to strike and occupy parts of the south.

stories.headline: Gaza ceasefire stalls as world looks elsewhere — humanitarian situation deteriorating
stories.summary: With international attention focused on the Iran war, the Gaza ceasefire process has slipped down the priority list and conditions on the ground are worsening, Chatham House warns. Israeli forces also said they killed Izz-al-Din al-Haddad, the commander of Hamas's militant wing, in a strike in western Gaza City.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: For Singapore tonight, the cost-of-living story is dominant: COE premiums leapt again, with Cat A closing at $124,790 — its sixth straight rise — and Cat E hitting $127,700, putting cars further out of reach for many. Weather-wise, expect afternoon thundery showers through the second half of May and pre-dawn Sumatra squalls on some days. On the policy front, HDB's June BTO exercise will introduce Pearl's Hill and Toa Payoh West projects and raise the Third Child Priority quota from 5% to 10%, while Singapore and Brunei sealed an energy cooperation pact.

stories.headline: COE premiums surge again — Cat A hits $124,790, sixth consecutive rise
stories.summary: Results from the first May 2026 COE bidding showed premiums racing higher across the board: Cat A (smaller cars) at $124,790, Cat B (larger cars) at $126,236, Cat C (commercial) at $87,479, Cat D (motorbikes) at $9,452 and Open Cat E at $127,700. The next bidding round opens on 18 May at noon, with results on 20 May.

stories.headline: MSS forecast: thundery afternoon showers and Sumatra squalls through late May
stories.summary: Meteorological Service Singapore says short-duration thundery showers will hit parts of the island on most afternoons through the second fortnight of May, sometimes extending into the evening. Sumatra squalls may bring widespread thundery showers with gusty winds in the pre-dawn and morning hours on a few days. No active haze advisory is in effect.

stories.headline: HDB's June BTO: Pearl's Hill, Toa Payoh West and a bigger Third Child quota
stories.summary: HDB will launch about 19,600 BTO flats across 2026, with new projects at Pearl's Hill and Toa Payoh West, plus a return to Lakeview and Shunfu — the first new public housing there in four decades. From the June exercise, the Third Child Priority Scheme quota doubles from up to 5% to up to 10% of flats per launch.

stories.headline: Singapore and Brunei sign deal to deepen energy cooperation
stories.summary: The two countries agreed to boost energy cooperation and shore up supply-chain resilience, deepening a partnership that already runs through ASEAN. The move comes as the region watches Middle East tensions and any knock-on effects on oil and LNG shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Permanent Secretary Pang Kin Keong to retire after 35 years
stories.summary: Veteran civil servant Pang Kin Keong, currently Permanent Secretary for Home Affairs, will retire from the Administrative Service on 1 June after 35 years in public service. His career spans senior postings across multiple ministries and statutory boards.

stories.headline: LRT service hit by issue between Bukit Panjang and Senja
stories.summary: Commuters faced disruption on the Bukit Panjang LRT after a service issue between Bukit Panjang and Senja stations earlier today. Bridging services were arranged as engineers worked to restore normal operations.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: Three theatres remain hot. The US-Israel war on Iran took its most alarming turn yet with a drone hitting the perimeter of the UAE's Barakah nuclear plant — Tehran denies involvement and back-channel talks via Pakistan are reportedly underway. In Ukraine, Russia is escalating with record drone barrages and a new ICBM test even as Putin floats peace talks — Zelenskyy is having none of it. The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was extended 45 days but is being violated daily, and Gaza is sliding deeper into a humanitarian crisis the world has stopped watching.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active — escalating air war alongside contradictory peace signals

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
War of attrition continues; both sides build out long-range drone and missile capabilities; Western military aid to Kyiv comes in fits and starts.
Russia launches a record 8,000+ drones in a single month — highest monthly total since the invasion began.
Russia and Ukraine each declare competing, partial ceasefires that neither side observes.
Putin says the war may be 'coming to an end' and offers to meet Zelenskyy in a third country.
Ukraine strikes gas facilities deep inside Russia's Orenburg region — over 1,500 km from the border.
Russia fires 55 missiles and 8,517 kamikaze drones in a single 24-hour barrage, the largest of the war.
Russia tests a new nuclear-capable ICBM; Putin says it will be deployed by year-end.
234 combat engagements reported in a day; 95 airstrikes and 300 guided aerial bombs dropped on Ukrainian positions.
Zelenskyy rejects Putin's framing — says Ukraine is preparing for further attacks and Russia 'has no intention of ending this war'.

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran War
theatres.status: Active — drone strike on UAE nuclear plant marks a dangerous new line

timeline.event: 12-Day War: Israel and Iran exchange strikes before a US-brokered ceasefire halts active fighting.
US and Israel launch fresh airstrikes on Iran, hitting military and government sites; multiple senior Iranian officials reportedly killed, including Supreme Leader Khamenei.
Iran responds with missile salvos on Israel; war spreads to Lebanon and Strait of Hormuz.
Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins after US-brokered talks.
Israel kills 24 in Lebanon; US still waiting on Iran's reply to its proposed deal.
Trump's China visit overshadowed by Iran war; Israel launches fresh attacks.
Iran says it will soon unveil a toll mechanism for the Strait of Hormuz; Lebanon ceasefire extended.
Drone strike sparks fire at the perimeter of the UAE's Barakah nuclear plant — first time a working nuclear site has been hit in this war.
Iran says talks continue through Pakistan; Trump warns Tehran 'the clock is ticking' on a deal.

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Fragile ceasefire in Lebanon, humanitarian collapse in Gaza

timeline.event: Hamas attack on Israel triggers war in Gaza; northern front opens with Hezbollah.
Multiple Gaza ceasefires negotiated and broken; Lebanon-Israel exchanges escalate.
Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins, but is violated almost from day one.
Lebanon ceasefire extended; Israel continues strikes; over 657 killed in Lebanon since the ceasefire began.
Izz-al-Din al-Haddad, commander of Hamas's military wing, killed in Israeli strike in western Gaza City.
Ceasefire extended by another 45 days in Washington talks; same day, Israeli strikes kill at least five in southern Lebanon.
Chatham House warns Gaza ceasefire has slipped off the international agenda as Iran war dominates attention; humanitarian conditions deteriorating.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius — the Dutch cruise ship at the centre of the Andes hantavirus cluster — docks in the Netherlands today, ending its onboard outbreak phase. As of mid-May there are 8 confirmed and 2 suspected cases with 3 deaths (2 confirmed Andes), and former passengers are now scattered across at least 12 countries including Singapore. WHO says the epidemic risk is low because Andes only really spreads in close, sustained contact, but case-fatality is high (20–40%) and the US CDC has gone to a Level 3 emergency response. The next few weeks of contact tracing will tell us whether this stays a contained cluster.

stories.headline: MV Hondius docks in Netherlands today, ending shipboard phase of Andes outbreak
stories.summary: After weeks at sea with a hantavirus cluster on board, the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius is scheduled to arrive at its final stop in the Netherlands today. Authorities have prepared dockside health screening and isolation protocols for any remaining symptomatic passengers and crew.

stories.headline: WHO: 8 confirmed cases, 3 deaths — but epidemic risk assessed as low
stories.summary: WHO's latest disease outbreak news lists 8 confirmed and 2 suspected cases tied to the MV Hondius cluster, with 3 deaths (2 confirmed Andes virus). The agency says risk of a wider epidemic is low because Andes typically requires close, sustained contact, although case-fatality in past outbreaks has ranged 20–40%.

stories.headline: Former passengers now hospitalised or quarantined in 12+ countries — Singapore included
stories.summary: Disembarked MV Hondius passengers are being monitored in Australia, Canada, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Saint Helena, Singapore, South Africa, Spain, Switzerland, Turkey and the United States. National health authorities, including Singapore's CDA, are running active contact tracing.

stories.headline: US CDC raises response to Level 3 — WHO hasn't declared a formal emergency level
stories.summary: The US Centers for Disease Control has classified its outbreak response at Level 3, signalling sustained agency-wide focus. The WHO has not declared a specific emergency level but has stressed Andes is unusual among hantaviruses for its documented human-to-human transmission.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Patch fast — this is one of the heaviest cycles in months. CISA's deadline to patch the actively-exploited Cisco SD-WAN zero-day (CVE-2026-20182) passed yesterday for US federal agencies. Microsoft's Patch Tuesday shipped 120 fixes including two CVSS 9.8 'wormable-class' RCEs in Netlogon and the Windows DNS Client that every defender should prioritise. On top of that there's an actively-exploited NGINX/F5 heap overflow with public PoC, a critical pre-auth RCE in Juniper PTX routers, an Exchange Server zero-day, and three Microsoft Defender LPE flaws — two still without a clean patch. Bottom line: domain controllers, edge routers and Defender boxes all need eyes-on this week.

stories.headline: Cisco SD-WAN zero-day exploited — CISA KEV deadline was yesterday
stories.summary: Cisco confirmed a critical authentication bypass in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller (CVE-2026-20182) was exploited as a zero-day to gain admin privileges on compromised devices. CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog and required US federal agencies to patch by 17 May 2026 — yesterday.

stories.headline: Windows Netlogon RCE — wormable-class flaw in this month's Patch Tuesday
stories.summary: A stack-based buffer overflow in Windows Netlogon lets an unauthenticated attacker run code on domain controllers. Microsoft flagged it as wormable: one compromised DC can be used to attack others without user interaction. Part of the May 2026 Patch Tuesday batch of 120 fixes.

stories.headline: Windows DNS Client heap overflow — every Windows machine affected
stories.summary: A heap-based buffer overflow in the Windows DNS Client can be triggered by a malicious DNS response, leading to remote code execution. Because virtually every Windows machine uses the DNS client, the patch footprint is huge — prioritise alongside the Netlogon fix.

stories.headline: NGINX/F5 heap overflow under active exploitation — public PoC available
stories.summary: An unauthenticated remote code execution flaw affects NGINX/F5 versions 0.6.27 through 1.30.0 via a heap buffer overflow. A public proof-of-concept exists and exploitation in the wild has been reported. Anyone running an older or unpatched NGINX edge box should upgrade immediately.

stories.headline: Critical pre-auth RCE in Juniper PTX routers — full device takeover
stories.summary: Juniper Networks PTX-series routers running Junos OS Evolved 25.4 (before 25.4R1-S1-EVO / 25.4R2-EVO) carry a critical pre-auth RCE via the On-Box Anomaly Detection framework, which is externally exposed by default. No active exploitation reported yet, but Singapore's CSA has issued an alert and rapid weaponisation is likely.

stories.headline: Exchange Server zero-day exploited in the wild — Microsoft working on fix
stories.summary: A spoofing/XSS issue in Exchange Server Subscription Edition, 2016 and 2019 is being abused in active attacks. Microsoft says a patch is in progress; defenders should hunt for the published indicators of compromise and tighten outbound mail policies in the meantime.

stories.headline: Three Microsoft Defender zero-days — two still unpatched
stories.summary: BlueHammer (CVE-2026-33825) and RedSun are local privilege escalation flaws in Microsoft Defender; UnDefend is a denial-of-service. All three have been seen exploited in the wild — BlueHammer since at least 10 April 2026. Microsoft patched BlueHammer last month; RedSun was silently addressed and UnDefend has no public fix.

stories.headline: Chrome zero-day in Dawn graphics layer exploited in the wild
stories.summary: A use-after-free vulnerability in Dawn — Chrome's graphics abstraction layer — was actively exploited when Chrome 146 shipped in late March. If you haven't restarted your browser since, do it now: the patch only takes effect after a relaunch.
